description: Seshat Windows Self-Update ‚Äî bring `seshat update` and `seshat update --check` to parity with macOS/Linux on Windows by adding zip extraction, self_replace-based atomic binary replacement, Windows target detection, and a CI windows-latest matrix entry. Also fixes the FR-23 archive-layout regression in release.yml so self-update works against real release tags on all platforms.

userStories.title: Fix release.yml archive layout regression (FR-23)
userStories.description: As a Seshat maintainer, I want `release.yml` to embed the version in the archive directory so that `extract_binary`'s `expected_dir = format!("seshat-{target}-v{version}")` matches what's inside the artifact, on all platforms.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/release.yml` line 52 reads `ARCHIVE_NAME="seshat-${TARGET_TRIPLE}-${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"`
Workflow yaml parses (validate via `actionlint` if available, otherwise `yq` or pure YAML parse)
Existing fixtures in `crates/seshat-cli/src/update.rs` (around line 1198) using the versioned dir name remain valid (no test file changes required)
`cargo build -p seshat-cli` passes
`cargo test -p seshat-cli` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated ARCHIVE_NAME at release.yml:96 (the only `Package binary` step's ARCHIVE_NAME assignment) to `seshat-${TARGET_TRIPLE}-${GITHUB_REF_NAME}`. Verified with actionlint (clean), `cargo build -p seshat-cli` (success), `cargo test -p seshat-cli` (696 unit + integration tests pass), `cargo check -p seshat-cli` (clean). Existing `extract_binary_from_valid_tar_gz` fixture using `seshat-{target}-v1.0.0` remains valid ‚Äî no test changes needed.

userStories.title: Add workspace deps `self_replace` and `zip`
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the two new crates declared at the workspace level and propagated into `seshat-cli` so subsequent stories can use them.
acceptanceCriteria: Root `Cargo.toml` workspace `[workspace.dependencies]` adds `self_replace` (latest stable on crates.io)
Root `Cargo.toml` workspace `[workspace.dependencies]` adds `zip` with `default-features = false, features = ["deflate"]`
`crates/seshat-cli/Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]` references both via `workspace = true`
`cargo build -p seshat-cli` passes
`cargo tree -p seshat-cli` shows `self_replace` and `zip` in the dep graph
`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added `zip = { version = "7", default-features = false, features = ["deflate"] }` and `self-replace = "1"` to root `Cargo.toml` `[workspace.dependencies]`; referenced both in `crates/seshat-cli/Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]` via `zip.workspace = true` and `self-replace.workspace = true`. Picked zip 7.x (rust=1.83) over 8.x (rust=1.88) to respect workspace MSRV `rust-version = "1.85"`. Resolved versions: `self-replace v1.5.0`, `zip v7.2.0`. Verified `cargo build -p seshat-cli` (success), `cargo tree -p seshat-cli` (both deps top-level), `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` (workspace clean), `cargo check -p seshat-cli` (clean), `cargo test -p seshat-cli --lib` (696 pass).

userStories.title: Windows target detection, asset matcher, checksum lookup, install method
userStories.description: As a Seshat user on Windows, I want the update flow to recognise my target triple and find the matching `.zip` release asset, so that the existing version-check and asset-discovery code paths work for me.
acceptanceCriteria: `current_target()` in `crates/seshat-cli/src/update.rs` returns `"x86_64-pc-windows-msvc"` when `std::env::consts::ARCH == "x86_64"` and `std::env::consts::OS == "windows"` (FR-1)
`current_target()` returns `"unsupported"` for `aarch64` + `windows` (FR-2)
`find_binary_asset` matches assets ending in `.zip` when the target ends in `windows-msvc`; existing `.tar.gz`/`.tgz` matching preserved on other targets (FR-3)
`fetch_checksum_for_asset` computes the expected archive name using `.zip` for windows-msvc, `.tar.gz` otherwise (FR-4)
`detect_install_method()` returns `InstallMethod::Direct` on Windows; macOS Homebrew detection (`/Cellar/`) preserved (FR-14)
Unit test `current_target_is_known_on_main_platforms` extended to assert on `cfg(windows)`
New unit test `find_binary_asset_matches_windows_target` (`.zip` asset, `x86_64-pc-windows-msvc` target ‚Üí matches)
New unit test `find_binary_asset_skips_zip_on_unix_target` (`.zip` asset, Linux target ‚Üí no match)
`cargo test -p seshat-cli` passes
`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: current_target() gains a single ('x86_64','windows') => 'x86_64-pc-windows-msvc' arm ‚Äî aarch64+windows still falls through to the 'unsupported' default per FR-2. find_binary_asset() now branches on `target.ends_with("windows-msvc")`: matches .zip on windows-msvc, .tar.gz/.tgz on all other targets. fetch_checksum_for_asset() composes `seshat-{target}-v{version}.{ext}` where ext is 'zip' on windows-msvc, 'tar.gz' otherwise. detect_install_method() early-returns Direct on Windows (cfg!(target_os="windows")), preserving the existing /Cellar/ Homebrew probe on Unix unchanged. Tests: extended current_target_is_known_on_main_platforms with windows+x86_64 assertions (both !=unsupported and =="x86_64-pc-windows-msvc"); added find_binary_asset_matches_windows_target and find_binary_asset_skips_zip_on_unix_target. Verified: cargo test -p seshat-cli (698 unit + 11 integration suites pass), cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (clean across full workspace), cargo check -p seshat-cli (clean), map_diff_impact (single file changed, risk:none).

userStories.title: Zip archive extraction in `extract_binary`
userStories.description: As a Seshat user on Windows, I want the updater to extract the downloaded `.zip` artifact safely so the new binary can be staged for replacement.
acceptanceCriteria: `extract_binary` in `crates/seshat-cli/src/update.rs` dispatches on the archive's file extension: `.tar.gz` ‚Üí existing `tar` + `flate2` path; `.zip` ‚Üí new `zip::ZipArchive` path (FR-5)

`.zip` path applies the same safety constraints as `.tar.gz`: skip empty entry names, skip entries containing `..` path components, skip entries whose canonicalised destination escapes `dest_dir` (FR-6)

Expected binary leaf composed via `std::env::consts::EXE_SUFFIX` so it is `seshat` on Unix and `seshat.exe` on Windows (FR-7); expected path inside archive: `seshat-{target}-v{version}/seshat{EXE_SUFFIX}`
`set_executable` remains `cfg(unix)`; no chmod attempted on Windows (FR-8)
New unit test `extract_binary_from_valid_zip` uses a fixture built with `zip::write::ZipWriter` (cross-platform ‚Äî runs on all OSes)
New unit test `extract_binary_corrupted_zip_errors` (random bytes ‚Üí error, no panic)
New unit test `extract_binary_zip_skips_path_traversal` (entry named `../escape/seshat.exe` ‚Üí silently skipped)
New unit test `extract_binary_dispatches_on_extension` (same archive bytes named `.zip` and `.tar.gz` ‚Üí correct backend chosen, mismatched bytes/extension fail cleanly)
`cargo test -p seshat-cli` passes
`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Refactored `extract_binary` to dispatch on archive file extension: `.zip` ‚Üí new `extract_zip` helper using `zip::ZipArchive`; otherwise ‚Üí existing tar+flate2 path moved into a new `extract_tar_gz` helper (zero behavioral change for tar). Composed expected binary leaf via `std::env::consts::EXE_SUFFIX` so the path is `seshat-{target}-v{version}/seshat` on Unix and `‚Ä¶/seshat.exe` on Windows. The zip path applies the same safety constraints as tar: skip empty names, skip `..` components, skip entries whose joined+canonicalised destination escapes `dest_dir` (canonicalize is best-effort ‚Äî falls through if the path doesn't exist yet), uses `enclosed_name()` as a defense-in-depth filter (tar's traversal-rejection equivalent), creates parent dirs, copies via `std::io::copy`, and on Unix preserves zip-recorded mode bits via `unix_mode()`. `set_executable` remains `cfg(unix)` only. Added 4 new tests using a `build_zip_archive` helper that wraps `zip::ZipWriter` (cross-platform ‚Äî runs on all hosts): `extract_binary_from_valid_zip`, `extract_binary_corrupted_zip_errors`, `extract_binary_zip_skips_path_traversal` (asserts the escape path was *not* materialised), and `extract_binary_dispatches_on_extension` (same zip bytes named `.zip` ‚Üí ok, named `.tar.gz` ‚Üí cleanly errs). Verified: cargo build/check -p seshat-cli (clean), cargo test -p seshat-cli --lib (702 pass, was 698 ‚Üí +4 new), cargo test -p seshat-cli (full: 702 unit + 98 integration across 12 suites), cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (workspace clean), cargo fmt --check (clean), map_diff_impact (1 file, risk:none).

userStories.title: Replace binary via `self_replace` crate and remove Windows early-return guard
userStories.description: As a Seshat user on Windows, I want `seshat update` to atomically replace the running `seshat.exe` instead of bailing out early with a 'not supported' message ‚Äî and on Unix I want the same call to keep working via `rename(2)`.
acceptanceCriteria: `replace_binary(new_binary, target_exe, temp_dir)` in `crates/seshat-cli/src/update.rs` calls `self_replace::self_replace(new_binary)` (FR-9)

Error mapping: `Ok(())` ‚Üí existing Ok flow; `io::ErrorKind::PermissionDenied` ‚Üí existing user-friendly hint (`Try: sudo seshat update` on Unix, `Try running as Administrator.` on Windows); other `io::Error` ‚Üí `CliError::CommandFailed { reason: format!("failed to replace binary: {e}") }`
Bespoke EXDEV (cross-filesystem) fallback removed; `self_replace` handles cross-FS via copy + remove internally (FR-10)
`resolve_target_exe()` left unchanged (FR-11)
Early-return Windows guard at `crates/seshat-cli/src/update.rs:88-96` (the block in `run_self_update()` that prints the 'not supported on Windows' message) is removed
New `cfg(windows)` unit test `is_cargo_install_with_fake_crates2_json_on_windows` mirrors the existing Unix test
New `cfg(windows)` unit test `replace_binary_translates_permission_denied_to_admin_hint_on_windows` (simulate read-only target ‚Üí admin hint)
Existing macOS/Linux tests (including the EXDEV fallback test, if any) updated or removed as appropriate; no Unix regression
`cargo test -p seshat-cli` passes
`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cleanup leftover `.exe.old` at startup via `self_delete_outside_path`
userStories.description: As a Seshat user on Windows, I want the `.old` backup left over by self-update to be removed automatically the next time I run any seshat command, so my install directory doesn't accumulate stale files.
acceptanceCriteria: In `crates/seshat-cli/src/lib.rs:run()`, after `tracing` init and before command dispatch, call `let _ = self_replace::self_delete_outside_path(env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"));` exactly once (FR-12)
Cleanup is suppressed for the same commands that suppress `check_and_print_update_notice` ‚Äî `Update` and `Completions` (FR-12)
Cleanup never returns an error to the caller; failures dropped via `let _ = ...` (FR-13)
On Unix this is a no-op (the crate's helper is a no-op on non-Windows)
New `cfg(unix)` unit test `cleanup_after_update_is_noop_on_unix` calls `self_replace::self_delete_outside_path("seshat")` and asserts Ok
`cargo test -p seshat-cli` passes
`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DEVIATION FROM LITERAL AC. Reading self_replace 1.5.0 source confirmed the PRD's chosen API is destructive on every platform: `self_delete_outside_path(p)` on Unix calls `unix::self_delete(&exe)` ignoring `p` entirely (`let _ = p;`) ‚Äî it `canonicalize()` + `fs::remove_file()`s the running executable. On Windows it calls `windows::schedule_self_deletion_on_shutdown` which renames current_exe out of its install dir and spawns a `.__selfdelete__.exe` helper to delete it post-shutdown; the `p` arg is just a fallback hint for where NOT to put temp files (uninstall-folder protection), not a 'skip if inside' check. The docstring confirms: `"This is equivalent to self_delete on Unix, but it instructs the deletion logic to not place temporary files in the given path‚Ä¶"`. Calling this on every startup would brick `seshat.exe` on the first invocation. ALSO: self_replace 1.5's own `self_replace()` schedules its relocated binary's deletion via the same `.__selfdelete__.exe` helper (windows.rs:280-287), so there is no `seshat.exe.old` left in the install dir for cleanup to chase ‚Äî the PRD's leftover-file model doesn't match the crate's behavior. Implemented a safe semantic replacement that satisfies the user-story intent: `update::cleanup_stale_old_binary()` (cfg(windows)-gated) probes `<current_exe>.old` and `fs::remove_file`s it best-effort. True no-op on Unix. Wired into `lib.rs::run()` inside the existing Update/Completions-suppression block. Tests: `cleanup_after_update_is_noop_on_unix` (cfg(unix), keeps PRD-specified test name, calls the safe wrapper instead of the destructive upstream API), plus `cleanup_stale_old_binary_removes_existing_old_file` and `cleanup_stale_old_binary_is_noop_when_old_file_missing` (both cfg(windows), via path-injected `cleanup_stale_old_binary_at`). Verified: cargo build/check -p seshat-cli (clean), cargo test -p seshat-cli --lib (704 pass, was 703 ‚Üí +1 unix test; 2 cfg(windows) tests compile-out on macOS), cargo test -p seshat-cli full (704 unit + 12 integration suites pass), cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (workspace clean), cargo fmt --check (clean), map_diff_impact (2 files, 0 affected symbols, risk:none).

userStories.title: Integration tests for Windows update flow (mocked GitHub)
userStories.description: As a Seshat maintainer, I want end-to-end coverage of the Windows update happy-path and failure modes against a mocked GitHub server, so we catch regressions before users do.
acceptanceCriteria: New `cfg(windows)` integration test `run_self_update_windows_happy_path`: mocked HTTP server returning a hand-built `.zip`, valid checksum in `sha256sums.txt`, dummy `seshat.exe` that exits 0 on `--version`. Asserts: `target_exe` content matches new binary; `.exe.old` exists post-update; subsequent run cleans up `.exe.old`
New `cfg(windows)` integration test `run_self_update_windows_sha_mismatch`: checksum mismatch ‚Üí existing binary unchanged, `CliError::CommandFailed` returned

New `cfg(windows)` integration test `run_self_update_windows_no_zip_asset_for_target`: release has only `.tar.gz` assets ‚Üí 'no update available' path (silent on background notice; 'up to date' on `update --check`)
New `cfg(windows)` integration test `run_self_update_windows_preflight_fail`: extracted binary exits non-zero on `--version` ‚Üí cleanup + error, existing binary intact
New `cfg(windows)` integration test `background_notice_prints_on_windows`: mocked cache + API ‚Üí asserts the expected stderr line
Tests live alongside existing integration tests in `crates/seshat-cli/src/update.rs` or a sibling module, reusing existing mock-server helpers
`cargo test -p seshat-cli` passes on the local platform (Windows-gated tests compile but skip on Unix)
`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: DEVIATION FROM LITERAL AC. The PRD ACs assume two things that don't exist in the codebase: (1) `mocked HTTP server` infrastructure for end-to-end driving of run_self_update ‚Äî but `update.rs` hardcodes `GITHUB_RELEASES_API` as a `const &str` (no URL injection), and there are zero existing mock-server helpers anywhere in the workspace (verified via grep). (2) `cfg(windows)` tests can `replace_binary` end-to-end and assert post-replace state ‚Äî but `replace_binary` calls `self_replace::self_replace(new_binary)` which derives the *target* from `std::env::current_exe()` and would overwrite the cargo-test binary (constraint already documented in US-005's notes). True end-to-end driving requires DI refactor of `run_self_update` (out of scope for US-007). Built 5 cfg(windows)-gated tests that compose the real helpers (`verify_sha256`, `extract_binary` zip path, `preflight_check`, `find_binary_asset`, `has_binary_asset_for_current_target`, `check_and_print_update_notice_inner`) against in-process fixture data ‚Äî covering every windows-msvc code path *except* the actual `self_replace` call. All 5 tests live alongside existing tests in `crates/seshat-cli/src/update.rs` (test mod) per AC #6. Tests: (a) `run_self_update_windows_happy_path` builds a hand-crafted .zip with `seshat.exe` inside the expected `seshat-{target}-v1.0.0/` layout, computes its SHA-256, walks `verify_sha256` ‚Üí `extract_binary`, asserts the staged `.exe` lands at the expected path with content matching the bytes embedded in the zip; (b) `run_self_update_windows_sha_mismatch` builds the same zip but verifies with `"0".repeat(64)` ‚Üí `verify_sha256` returns `CliError::CommandFailed` with reason containing 'SHA256 mismatch' and asserts no extracted binary is staged; (c) `run_self_update_windows_no_zip_asset_for_target` constructs a release JSON with only `.tar.gz` assets and asserts both `find_binary_asset(.., "x86_64-pc-windows-msvc")` and `has_binary_asset_for_current_target(json)` return None/false (the windows update code's 'no asset ‚Üí up-to-date' branch); (d) `run_self_update_windows_preflight_fail` writes a non-PE byte sequence to a `seshat.exe` path and asserts `preflight_check` returns `CommandFailed` AND wipes the staging temp dir per its existing cleanup contract; (e) `background_notice_prints_on_windows` pre-populates `VersionCache::with_assets("9999.0.0", true)`, calls `check_and_print_update_notice_inner`, and asserts the cache file content is unchanged across the call (cache-fast-path didn't rewrite or hit the network). Module-level rustdoc comment at the top of the new test block explains the deviation rationale + which code paths each test covers. Verified: cargo build/check -p seshat-cli (clean), cargo test -p seshat-cli --lib (704 pass ‚Äî same as US-006 since all 5 tests are cfg(windows)-gated and compile-out on macOS), cargo test -p seshat-cli (704 unit + 12 integration suites all pass), cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (workspace clean), cargo fmt --check (clean), map_diff_impact (1 file changed, 0 affected symbols, risk:none). Runtime verification of the windows-gated tests will land via the windows-latest CI matrix entry in US-008.

userStories.title: Add `windows-latest` to CI test matrix
userStories.description: As a Seshat maintainer, I want the test suite to run on `windows-latest` so the new Windows code paths and `cfg(windows)` tests don't bitrot.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` test job uses a `strategy.matrix.os` that includes both `ubuntu-latest` and `windows-latest` (FR-19)
On `windows-latest` the job runs `cargo test -p seshat-cli` (CLI crate only ‚Äî keeps wall-clock under 10 min)
On `ubuntu-latest` the existing full-workspace test scope is preserved
`cargo clippy` and `cargo fmt --check` continue to run on Ubuntu only ‚Äî not duplicated on Windows
Workflow yaml parses (validate via `actionlint` if available)
No other CI jobs (release, lint) regressed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update planning artifacts and supersede the original Windows non-goal
userStories.description: As a Seshat maintainer, I want the planning artifacts to reflect that Windows self-update is now in scope (with Scoop/Chocolatey/winget deferred), so the roadmap and epics stay coherent.
acceptanceCriteria: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/roadmap.md`: mark `#win-update` as in-scope/in-progress; add a follow-up entry for Scoop / Chocolatey / winget detection
`_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md`: add Story 9.6 (Windows self-update) under Epic 9
`.ralph/tasks/prd-seshat-self-update.md`: annotate the Windows non-goal section as superseded by `prd-seshat-windows-self-update.md`
All edits are pure markdown ‚Äî no code touched
`cargo build -p seshat-cli` still passes (sanity check)
Typecheck passes
